title: hieracium cydoniifolium subsp. subpanduratum

parts: Hieracium cydoniifolium subsp. subpanduratum has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and flowers. The leaves of Hieracium cydoniifolium subsp. subpanduratum are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and flowers of this plant are reported to contain sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, which have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. side_effects: Hieracium cydoniifolium subsp. subpanduratum can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged consumption may also lead to dizziness, headaches, and stomach ulcers. In severe cases, excessive use can cause damage to the liver and kidneys, as well as interact with blood thinners and other medications. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using Hieracium cydoniifolium subsp. subpanduratum for medicinal purposes.
